name: Ostrich Chili-Corn Tamale (La Times)
recipeIngredient: 10 Ancho chiles, peeled and seeded, or 10 tablespoons ground ancho
8 corn husks, tied on each end
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak chiles in a bowl of hot water to cover until soft.
2. Place the chiles and 2 tablespoons of soaking liquid from the chiles in a blender or food processor. Puree for 1 minute. If using ground chiles, mix with 2 tablespoons of water.
3. Add cumin, coriander, oregano, and cinnamon to the puree and continue blending until smooth.
4. Heat oil in a saucepan until very hot. Add the chile puree and fry for 1 minute.
5. Add onion, garlic, ostrich, and torn tortilla. Stir well to combine.
6. Add beer and stock, bring to boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 2 hours.
7. Remove stew from heat and season to taste with salt and lime juice.
8. Spread 1 1/2 tablespoons of corn puree on each corn husk "boat."
9. Top with stew and serve.
Makes 8 servings.
